purpose: Defines the `!deps [dashboardId] [task_id] [--critical] [--blocked] | validate` command for dependency graph visualization and validation. Three modes: full graph (ASCII visualization with status indicators, critical path, bottlenecks), single-task trace (upstream and downstream chains), and `validate` subcommand which runs five structural checks (circular detection via Kahn's algorithm, broken references, wave consistency, island detection, completeness/agent-wave count match). Read-only — produces no artifacts.
gotchas: Wave consistency check is WARN-level (non-blocking) — same-wave or reverse-wave dependencies may be intentional but typically indicate planning errors
Island detection EXCLUDES Wave 1 tasks (they're expected to have no deps) — only flags non-root disconnected tasks
Validation Overall status: PASS = no warnings/failures; WARN = warnings only; FAIL = any failure (one FAIL trumps any number of PASSes)
Argument disambiguation: first non-flag arg may be either a dashboard ID or a task ID — task IDs contain a dot (e.g., `2.3`) while dashboard IDs do not
patterns: Three command modes share Steps 1-3 (parse → read init → read progress) then diverge
ASCII graph rendering uses ──→ arrows and ┘ joins; status emojis on each node (✅🔵⚪🔴)
Critical path = longest chain from any root to any terminal task
Bottleneck identification = in-progress tasks with most downstream waiters
Validate subcommand: build adjacency lists (forward + backward edges) then run all 5 checks in sequence
Validation output: 5-row results table, then detail messages grouped by check number under Warnings/Failures sub-headers
conventions: Section per command mode (full graph, single task, validate)
Validation checks numbered 1-5 with PASS/WARN/FAIL outcomes per check
Result-table convention: rows = checks, columns = check name + result
